disclosure: Generated by FoxClaw for CoinFox. Public links and summaries only. Not individualized advice.

source_window.summary: Manual beta fixture covering social heat, prediction-market movement, and public market context.

cards.title: WEN retail attention is becoming a thesis candidate
cards.why_interesting: Retail attention can appear before a mainstream narrative, but it needs hard evidence before it becomes a thesis.
cards.public_safe_summary: WEN is a candidate for a CoinFox discussion prompt because social attention is rising around a possible turnaround or meme-stock story.
cards.counterpoint: Social heat can fade quickly and does not prove price follow-through, valuation support, or business improvement.
cards.suggested_thesis_angle: Is WEN only a social spark, or is there enough price, volume, filing, or leadership evidence to form a real thesis?
cards.suggested_coinfox_prompt: What would make the WEN setup real enough to watch, and what would prove it was only heat?

outcome_memory.review_question: Did social heat lead price and volume, or did the narrative fade?

cards.title: Prediction-market odds shift needs source context
cards.why_interesting: Odds moves can reveal changing crowd expectations before the surrounding news is easy to summarize.
cards.public_safe_summary: A prediction-market move is worth a CoinFox prompt when probability changes faster than the public narrative explains.
cards.counterpoint: Thin liquidity, sports/news timing, or market structure can make an odds move look more meaningful than it is.
cards.suggested_thesis_angle: What public information explains the probability move, and is the market reacting to real evidence or temporary flow?
cards.suggested_coinfox_prompt: What changed in the odds, what public source explains it, and what would reverse the move?

outcome_memory.review_question: Did the odds move anticipate public evidence or revert after thin flow?

cards.title: BTC discussion shifted from price chase to invalidation
cards.why_interesting: A useful daily delta is not another headline; it shows how the market question changed.
cards.public_safe_summary: The useful BTC prompt is moving from direction to conditions: what level, catalyst, or macro change would invalidate the current thesis?
cards.counterpoint: A cleaner question does not mean the setup is tradeable or low risk.
cards.suggested_thesis_angle: Shift BTC discussion toward invalidation, evidence, and risk instead of chasing the latest candle.
cards.suggested_coinfox_prompt: What changed since yesterday, and what would make your BTC view wrong?

outcome_memory.review_question: Did users name invalidation levels before the next major move?
